concept: Design / Specify Intended Form, Relations, and Function
description: To specify what a thing should be: its form, its relations, its function, its way of being made. A design can be partial, provisional, or shared, and the exact drawing that carries its dimensions is source material beside the Phi. A design is not yet the made thing; 'pilewa' (make) is the second act.
sound_symbolism: Rounded 'no' starts with a dental nasal, and stressed 'po' repeats its round vowel after a full closure of the lips. A rolling tongue breaks the paired enclosures in final 'ru' before the sound sinks into deep 'u'. Twice the word gathers itself; only at the end does it move.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'mia ha rolu noporu.' (I design the wheel.) The event-noun rule supplies a design. State intended function, users, materials, constraints, evidence, and revision status where relevant. Distinct from 'norelu' (form), 'sena' (pattern), 'winora' (plan), 'kisholu' (specification), and 'pilewa' (make).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Repairable and ecologically situated systems require design choices whose users, materials, dependencies, and consequences can be named and revised.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Design can integrate function, material, structure, and living form rather than treating ornament as an afterthought.

tags.activity: specifying an intended artifact, system, or intervention
tags.aesthetic: shaping form in relation to material and use
tags.cognition: developing and selecting among possible relations
tags.creation: guiding making through an intentional specification
